skill.name: app-builder
skill.description: Main application building orchestrator that creates full-stack applications from natural language requests, determines project type, selects tech stack, and coordinates specialist agents.
skill.summary: Orchestrates full-stack application development by analyzing requirements, selecting technologies, and coordinating specialist AI agents.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 195 patterns but all are false positives. The skill consists of Markdown documentation files, not executable code. Backtick patterns are Markdown code fences for displaying examples, not shell execution. Environment variable references document standard project structure. No malicious patterns detected. External commands risk factor retained because the skill instructs AI agents to run bash commands for project scaffolding and dependency installation.

low_findings.title: External Command Execution via Agent
low_findings.description: Skill instructs AI agents to execute shell commands for project scaffolding, package installation, and database migrations. Commands include npm install, npx prisma, and create-next-app.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Skill explicitly documents shell commands for project setup - legitimate development workflow but requires user trust in executed commands.

content.user_title: Build Full-Stack Applications with AI Agent Coordination
content.value_statement: Transform natural language requests into production-ready applications. App Builder analyzes your requirements, selects the optimal tech stack, and coordinates specialist AI agents to build complete projects from scratch.
actual_capabilities: Analyzes natural language requirements to determine project type and tech stack
Coordinates multiple specialist agents including database architect, backend specialist, frontend specialist, and DevOps engineer
Scaffolds 13 different project types including Next.js, Nuxt, Express, FastAPI, React Native, Flutter, Electron, and Chrome extensions
Creates comprehensive development plans with task breakdown and dependency graphs
Implements feature-by-feature development with error handling and recovery strategies
Sets up preview deployments and validates health checks
limitations: Requires user approval before executing shell commands or installing dependencies
Does not directly execute code - operates as an orchestration and documentation layer
Project detection relies on keyword matching and may require clarification for ambiguous requests
Specialist agents must complete tasks sequentially with mandatory verification checkpoints

use_cases.title: Rapid SaaS Product Development
use_cases.description: Build a complete SaaS application with authentication, payment processing, and dashboard from a single description. App Builder coordinates database setup, API routes, frontend components, and Stripe integration.
use_cases.target_user: Startup founders and indie developers launching MVPs quickly

use_cases.title: Multi-Platform Mobile App Creation
use_cases.description: Generate React Native or Flutter applications with state management, navigation, and API integration. Choose between Expo for rapid prototyping or bare workflow for native modules.
use_cases.target_user: Mobile developers targeting iOS and Android from one codebase

use_cases.title: Enterprise Monorepo Scaffolding
use_cases.description: Set up Turborepo-powered monorepos with shared packages, apps, and CI-CD pipelines. Configure path aliases, shared types, and deployment workflows across multiple applications.
use_cases.target_user: Engineering teams managing multiple related applications

prompt_templates.title: Simple Web App Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a blog platform where users can write articles, add tags, and comment. Include user authentication and an admin dashboard for content management.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner-friendly request for a common web application type

prompt_templates.title: E-Commerce Platform Specification
prompt_templates.prompt: Build an e-commerce site with product catalog, shopping cart, Stripe checkout, order history, and email notifications. Use Next.js for the frontend and PostgreSQL for the database. Include inventory management and admin panel.
prompt_templates.scenario: Detailed specification with explicit tech stack requirements

prompt_templates.title: API-First Backend Service
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a REST API for a task management system with user authentication, project workspaces, task assignments, and real-time notifications. Use Express.js with JWT auth, PostgreSQL, and Redis for caching. Include OpenAPI documentation and unit tests.
prompt_templates.scenario: Backend-focused project with specific architectural requirements

prompt_templates.title: Cross-Platform Desktop Application
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a note-taking desktop app using Electron with React. Features include markdown editing, folder organization, full-text search, and cloud sync. Store data locally with SQLite and provide optional Dropbox integration for backup.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced desktop application with offline-first architecture

output_examples.input: Create a portfolio website with a project gallery, about page, and contact form
output: Tech Stack: Next.js 16, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Framer Motion
Files Generated: 12 components, 4 pages, configuration files

output_examples.input: Build a real-time chat application with rooms and direct messaging
output: Tech Stack: Next.js, PostgreSQL, Prisma, Supabase Realtime
Agents Coordinated: Database Architect, Backend Specialist, Frontend Specialist
Features Implemented: User auth, chat rooms, 1:1 messaging, message history, typing indicators
Deployment: Configured Vercel with Supabase integration

best_practices: Always review the generated plan file before specialist agents begin implementation
Specify your preferred tech stack in the initial request to avoid clarification rounds
Run preview deployments frequently to validate functionality during development
anti_patterns: Skipping the plan verification checkpoint leads to misaligned implementation
Requesting multiple unrelated features simultaneously reduces code quality
Ignoring environment variable setup causes runtime failures in database and auth

faq.question: How does App Builder determine which project template to use?
faq.answer: App Builder uses keyword matching against a matrix of 15+ project types. For example, 'blog' or 'documentation' triggers the Astro static template, while 'e-commerce' or 'payment' selects Next.js SaaS. Ambiguous requests prompt clarifying questions.

faq.question: Can I customize the default tech stack?
faq.answer: Yes, specify your preferred technologies in the initial request. App Builder defaults to Next.js 16, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and PostgreSQL, but you can request alternatives like FastAPI, Flutter, or Express.

faq.question: Does App Builder execute code on my machine?
faq.answer: App Builder generates commands and instructions for AI agents to execute. Commands like npm install or database migrations require user approval. The skill itself is documentation that guides the development workflow.

faq.question: How long does it take to scaffold a complete project?
faq.answer: Basic projects scaffold in 5-10 minutes. Full-stack applications with authentication, database, and multiple features typically complete in 15-30 minutes depending on complexity and number of specialist agents involved.

faq.question: Can App Builder add features to existing projects?
faq.answer: Yes, the feature-building workflow analyzes existing codebases, creates change plans, and implements enhancements iteratively. It detects project structure and follows established patterns.

faq.question: What happens if an error occurs during development?
faq.answer: App Builder implements automatic error recovery: detecting errors, attempting fixes, suggesting alternatives, and rolling back changes if necessary. Common issues like TypeScript errors or missing dependencies have predefined solutions.
